prs.title: feat: enhance Report Viewer with dynamic scoring visuals and Next Steps section
prs.body:
**Overview**

This PR enhances the ReportViewer component to provide a more personalized and informative experience for users after completing an assessment. It introduces dynamic styling based on performance levels and adds a comprehensive "Next Steps" guide.

**Key Changes**

Dynamic Performance Visuals:

- Updated the report bars to pull their colors directly from the custom scoringScale defined during assessment creation.
- Improved the visual weight of the performance level labels (e.g., Average, Moderate) for better readability.

Next Steps Section:
- Added a new "Next Steps" card that appears for standard assessments.
- Included information about support services like HappiGUIDE, HappiLEARN, HappiBUDDY, HappiSELF, and HappiTALK.
- Contact & Support: Integrated official contact details (email and phone) and a direct link to the Happimynd website.
- Disclaimer & Guidelines: Added a professional disclaimer section with references to ICD-10 and DSM-5® guidelines, along with national emergency helpline numbers.

issues.title: Bug: Editing a question in a packet removes it on reload
issues.body:
Description
When a question is added to a packet and then edited (via the edit/save flow), the question disappears from the packet after a page reload. The question still exists in the database but becomes orphaned (not associated with any packet).

Steps to Reproduce
1. Log in as admin
2. Navigate to Packet Management
3. Select an existing packet (or create a new one)
4. Add a new question to the packet
5. Click the Edit (pencil) icon on the newly added question
6. Make any change (or no change at all) and click Save
7. Reload the page
8. The edited question is no longer visible under the packet

Expected Behavior
The question should remain associated with the packet after editing and saving, and should persist across page reloads.

Actual Behavior
The question disappears from the packet on reload. It still exists in the data store but with packet_id: null, so it is not matched to any packet.

issues.title: Missing navigation button on "Write a Letter" page to return to Home
issues.body:
Description

Issue:
When a user navigates to the Write a Letter section, there is no visible button or link that allows them to return to the Home page. This results in poor navigation flow and user experience.

Steps to Reproduce:
Go to the website.
Navigate to the Write a Letter section.
Observe that there is no button or link to go back to the Home page.

Expected Behavior:
There should be a "Back to Home" or "Home" button/link on the Write a Letter page for easy navigation.

Suggested Fix:
Add a navigation element (button or link) that redirects users to the Home page, ideally placed at the top or bottom of the Write a Letter section.


Additional Context:
This fix will improve user accessibility and overall UX consistency across the website.

issues.title: Feature: Improve header styling for active page indication and enhance heading text on “Trending Now” page
issues.body:
### 📝 Summary

Add visual indication in the header to highlight the current active page and improve the heading design/text on the “Trending Now” page for better clarity and UI consistency.

### 🎯 Motivation / User Problem

Currently, the website header does not visually show which page the user is on — all navigation links look the same.
This makes navigation slightly confusing and less interactive.
Also, the “Trending Now” page heading feels plain and lacks emphasis, reducing its visual impact compared to other sections.

Improving these two elements will make the UI more intuitive and engaging for users.

### 👤 User Stories

As a user, I want to see which page I’m currently on so that I can navigate the site more confidently.

As a user, I want the “Trending Now” heading to look more attractive and prominent so that I immediately know I’m viewing trending content

### 🧩 Proposed Solution / Design Notes

Add an active state style to the current page link in the header (e.g., underline, color highlight, or background accent).

Refine the “Trending Now” heading text with consistent font weight, spacing, or a small accent element (like an icon or underline) to make it stand out.

Use shared theme variables so that the design remains consistent across light and dark modes.

### 🔁 Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### 🔼 Priority

Medium

### 🧠 Additional Context

This enhancement will improve overall site navigation clarity and visual consistency, particularly helpful for first-time visitors or users browsing multiple pages quickly.

issues.title: Bug: Inconsistent dark mode styling in “Our Work” section (search bar and header design mismatch)
issues.body: ### Describe the bug Describe the bug When switching to dark mode in the “Our Work” section, the search bar styling looks inconsistent with the rest of the theme. Additionally, the header design does not match other pages — spacing, background color, and text contrast differ, making the UI look uneven. ### To Reproduce To Reproduce 1. Open the GrowCraft website. 2. Navigate to the “Our Work” section. 3. Toggle dark mode. 4. Observe the search bar and header styling compared to other sections/pages. ### Expected behavior Expected behavior The dark mode design for the “Our Work” section should maintain consistent header styling and uniform search bar theme like the rest of the website. ### Additional context Additional context Ensuring consistent dark mode styling will improve the visual flow and UX across all sections. Possible fix: Adjust dark mode CSS variables or shared layout components for the “Our Work” section to match the global design theme.

issues.title: Bug: Movie list page scrolls when viewing movie info
issues.body:
### Describe the bug

When a user clicks on any movie to view its details and scrolls down, the main movie list page in the background also scrolls.
This causes a confusing user experience — the background content should remain fixed when the movie info section or modal is open.


### Environment

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the website and go to the Movies section.
2. Click on any movie to open its info/details view.
3. Try to scroll down inside the info section.
4. Notice that the background movie list page also scrolls along with it.

### Expected behavior

The background movie list page should not scroll when the user is viewing detailed information about a movie. Only the movie info container should scroll independently (if it has overflow content).

### Actual behavior

While viewing a movie’s info section, scrolling down also moves the main movie list page in the background, leading to unwanted content movement.

### Logs / Stack traces

```shell

```

### Severity

None

### Additional context

This issue might be caused by scroll not being locked on the background container when the info section or modal opens.
A potential fix is to disable body scroll (e.g., overflow: hidden on <body>) when the info view is active.

issues.title: Fix: Infinite scrolling prevents footer access — add "Load More" button instead
issues.body:
Description
🐛 Problem Statement

Currently, the website uses infinite scrolling to load more movies. However, this causes a major usability issue — users can never reach the footer section, as new content keeps loading continuously. This prevents access to important footer links and information (like contact, about, or policies).

💡 Proposed Solution

Replace the infinite scrolling feature with a "Load More" button at the bottom of the page.
Display an initial limited number of movies (e.g., 20).
When the user clicks the "Load More" button, load the next batch of movies.
Stop loading when no more movies are available and hide the button.


📱 Additional Context
This fix will improve accessibility, allow users to reach the footer easily, and give them more control over loading content.

issues.title: Feature Request: Add Dark Mode / Light Mode Toggle
issues.body:
## Problem Statement
Currently, the website only supports a light theme.  
Users who prefer a darker interface, especially when browsing at night, might find the current design too bright and straining on the eyes.  
Adding a dark mode option would enhance accessibility and user comfort.

## Proposed Solution
Introduce a **Dark Mode / Light Mode toggle button** that allows users to switch between themes.  

### Suggested Implementation:
- Add a toggle (switch icon or moon/sun button) in the header or settings section.
- Store user preference in **localStorage** so that the theme persists on reload.
- Apply a global CSS class (e.g., `.dark-mode`) to handle dark color variables.
- Use CSS variables or a theme context to switch between light and dark themes.

Example (pseudo-code):
```js
const [isDarkMode, setIsDarkMode] = useState(false);

useEffect(() => {
  document.body.classList.toggle('dark-mode', isDarkMode);
}, [isDarkMode]);

issues.title: Bug: Missing Icon in "Privacy First" Column on Home Page
issues.body: ## Description On the Home page, under the **"Everything You Need for Digital Productivity"** section, the icon in the **"Privacy First"** column is not rendering. The text and layout appear correctly, but the icon that should accompany the "Privacy First" label is missing. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to the Home page of the website. 2. Scroll down to the **"Everything You Need for Digital Productivity"** section. 3. Observe the **"Privacy First"** column. 4. Notice that no icon is displayed next to the heading. ## Expected behavior The **"Privacy First"** column should display a corresponding icon (similar to other columns in the same section), maintaining visual consistency. ## Screenshots - The section where the missing icon should appear. - Comparison with other columns (that display icons correctly). ## Environment - Extension version: N/A - Chrome version: 118.0.5993.90 - OS: macOS Sonoma - Backend running? (yes/no): yes ## Additional context The icon might be missing due to: - Incorrect or missing import path. - Broken SVG or font-awesome reference. - Typo in the icon class name or JSX element. I’d like to fix this issue under **Hacktoberfest 2025** by identifying the missing icon reference and restoring consistent UI alignment.

issues.title: Fix Copy Button: Add "Copied!" Feedback After Copy Action
Enhance Header: Add Logo and Improve Header Design
issues.body:
## 📝 Description
Currently, the header of the app only displays the plain text **"Movie App"**.  
To make the interface more visually appealing and professional, I propose enhancing the header by adding:
- A movie-themed logo (e.g., film reel, clapperboard, or play icon)
- Better typography or font style for the title
- Improved spacing and alignment

## 🎨 Proposed Enhancements
- Add a small logo image/icon next to the app name.
- Use CSS styling (or a styled component) to center and modernize the header.
- Optional: Add a simple hover animation or color gradient for aesthetics.
- Ensure it remains **responsive** across devices (mobile, tablet, desktop).

## 💻 Files to Modify
Likely files involved:
- `src/components/NavBar.jsx`
- `src/css/NavBar.css` 

## ✅ Expected Outcome
- Header displays a logo + stylized app name.
- Responsive and visually consistent across screen sizes.
- Overall improved look and feel.

## 🧑‍💻 Additional Notes
I’d like to work on this issue as part of **Hacktoberfest 2025** if approved.
